methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $600M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $800M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Profluent, a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Ali Madani, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health. The target currently carries a $600M post-money valuation following $150M in total capital raised.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.1B. Peer-median triangulation across four same-stage biotech companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), and Asimov ($800M)—produces a median of $1.3B, with a range spanning $800M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while Biotech & Health public peers trade at a 5.8x median P/S multiple across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L, Profluent's revenue remains undisclosed, precluding a revenue-multiple methodology.

The triangulated valuation range is $969M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median estimate of $1.2B. This implies an upside of approximately 100% from the current $600M valuation. Key uncertainties center on revenue trajectory and clinical-stage risk, both unresolved in available data. The wide dispersion in peer valuations ($800M–$3.3B) suggests material heterogeneity in company risk profiles within this stage and sector cohort.
